02 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 1989 1988 1987 1986 1985 1984 Skip to sidebar Gabriel: A Design Environment for Programmable DSPs by E.A. Lee, E. Goei, H. Heine, W.Ho, S. Bhattacharyya, J. Bier, E. Guntvedt Proc. of IEE

. Bhattacharyya, J. Bier, E. Guntvedt Proc. of IEEE Design Automation Conf. , pp. 141-146, Las Vegas, NV, June, 1989. July 19, 1999 Prepublished version Published version ABSTRACT Gabriel is a retargetable software system for the development of ass

getable software system for the development of assembly code and microcode for single or multiple programmable DSPs. It is intended to ease code development even for processors that are not easy targets for conventional compilers. Code generation for

ts for conventional compilers. Code generation for the Motorola DSP56001 is emphasized. A Thor-based simulator supplies a variety of target multi-DSP architectures based on the DSP56001. The top-level algorithm description is a large grain data frow

algorithm description is a large grain data frow graph, and a graphical interface using OCT and VEM provides a natural representation of the high level structure of the algorithm. Send comments to Professor Edward A. Lee .
